title: The Prototype Depth vs. Breadth Spectrum
description: A visual representation of the trade-off between prototype depth (level of interactivity and detail) and breadth (number of features covered), showing how different types of prototypes fit on this spectrum.
relationships: Low-Fidelity prototypes are broad and shallow
High-Fidelity prototypes can be either broad (horizontal) or deep (vertical)
A vertical prototype represents a single user flow with high depth
A horizontal prototype represents many features with low depth
